description_long: This utility pole is a tall, cylindrical structure made primarily of dark wood, featuring three horizontal crossarms stacked vertically near the top. Each crossarm supports a series of evenly spaced ceramic insulators, which are used to hold electrical wires. The pole itself has a series of metal steps or rungs running vertically up its lower section, likely for maintenance access. The insulators and crossarms are symmetrically arranged, and the pole appears robust and weather-resistant, suitable for outdoor urban environments. There are no visible transformers, streetlights, or identification tags attached. The overall design is functional and typical for supporting multiple power or communication lines in a city or neighborhood setting.
description: A tall, dark wooden utility pole with three stacked horizontal crossarms, each supporting multiple ceramic insulators and vertical metal rungs for climbing.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full face of the pole, all three crossarms with insulators, and the vertical climbing rungs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side profile view, where the pole appears as a thin line and the crossarms and insulators are barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, showing the crossarms, insulators, and climbing rungs from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the pole as a thin line with minimal detail visible.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: Three horizontal crossarms are stacked vertically near the top of the pole, evenly spaced and parallel to each other.
